evidence_quotes.quote: Is it ok for mobile games to make huge amounts of money from in-app purchases (mainly chance based tickets/currency) and leave the consumers with nothing at the end?
Like many mobile games, once services end, nothing remains for the consumers.
F.x Nintendo wants to control second hand markets of games, they want to disable internet access and remove games, that the owner has backed up themselves.
How long can we 'own' the games we purchase?

paragraphs.text: I'm concerned with how games are nowadays moving digital only, with limited physical releases and ownage. Games are discontinued whenever the publishers wishes, like Kingdom hearts Missing link. It was a free to play mobile game, with real money purchases. Like many mobile games, once services end, nothing remains for the consumers. Console games share a similar future, like the Crew. Once servers shut down, the whole game gets made unplayable, even physical disc releases. F.x Nintendo wants to control second hand markets of games, they want to disable internet access and remove games, that the owner has backed up themselves. How long can we 'own' the games we purchase? Is it ok for mobile games to make huge amounts of money from in-app purchases (mainly chance based tickets/currency) and leave the consumers with nothing at the end?
